source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $CD$ is the median to the hypotenuse $AB$. If $\angle A=20^\circ$, what is the measure of $\angle BDC$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given that $\angle ACB=90^\circ$ and $CD$ is the median to the hypotenuse $AB$,\\
it follows that $BD=CD=AD$,\\
therefore, $\angle A=\angle DCA=20^\circ$,\\
thus, $\angle BDC=\angle A+\angle DCA=20^\circ+20^\circ=\boxed{40^\circ}$.
